name: Billy Bauer
abstract: William Henry Bauer (November 14, 1915 – June 17, 2005) was an American jazz guitarist.

interviews.title: Billy Bauer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: "By mid-year the brilliant rhythm section of pianist Ralph Burns, bassist Chubby Jackson, guitarist Billy Bauer and drummer Dave Tough was in place."
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: When you mix other fellas like Billy Bauer, who is a genius in his own right in any phase of musical arrangement, along with the very healthy marriage that somehow or another came about with the Neal Hefti-Basie marriage there.

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Billy Bauer and Don Arnone, a lot of the old timers that I used to work with in the studios... when we started doing those rock & roll dates, every one — Mundell Lowe, Billy Bauer, Chuck Wayne, Tony Mottola, Al Caiola
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: I had a picture -- there was quite a few fellas on there Harry Edison reads personnel -- Tiny Grimes and Billy Bauer on guitar. ... No, I don't even know Billy Barrel... No

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: So we used to worry about Billy. But he was full of ideas and some of the earliest sounds we did, like, some of the small group things

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: the only time when I actually ever produced a bebop concert... which featured Lenny Tristano and Fats Navarro and, uh, Billy Bauer
